workItem.title: Re-evaluate terminal AgentRuns after an earlier no-op reconciliation
workItem.body:
<!-- bos:work-item {"acceptanceCriteria":["Repeated transport attempts in one reconciliation cycle share an operationId","A later reconciliation cycle receives a different operationId","Closed Symphony Issues 20 through 23 no longer expose running AgentRun projections","No duplicate terminal events or pull requests are created","The full Symphony gate passes with 100 percent coverage and zero Dialyzer findings"],"autonomy":"full","context":"Live acceptance after merging Symphony issues 20 through 23 found their GitHub Issues closed while their durable AgentRun projections remained running. The reconciliation endpoint had already seen those runs while their Issues were open, and the permanently stable operationId caused later post-merge calls to replay the earlier no-op response.","dependencies":[],"expectedOutcome":"Each reconciliation cycle uses a fresh idempotency scope while all retries inside that cycle retain one operation identity, allowing a run that becomes terminal after an earlier inspection to be finalized exactly once.","kind":"issue","operationId":"create_symphony_reconciliation_cycle_20260721","outOfScope":["Bypass game-api idempotency","Infer terminal state locally","Access GitHub directly from Symphony"],"parentIssueNumber":null,"risk":"high","scope":["Generate one unique reconciliation cycle identity per scheduler invocation","Reuse that identity for request retries within the invocation","Include the cycle identity in every terminal reconciliation operationId","Verify previously inspected running projections become terminal after their Issues close","Run the complete Symphony gate and a live restart acceptance"],"status":"ready","title":"Re-evaluate terminal AgentRuns after an earlier no-op reconciliation"} -->

## Context

Live acceptance after merging Symphony issues 20 through 23 found their GitHub Issues closed while their durable AgentRun projections remained running. The reconciliation endpoint had already seen those runs while their Issues were open, and the permanently stable operationId caused later post-merge calls to replay the earlier no-op response.

## Expected outcome

Each reconciliation cycle uses a fresh idempotency scope while all retries inside that cycle retain one operation identity, allowing a run that becomes terminal after an earlier inspection to be finalized exactly once.

## Scope

- Generate one unique reconciliation cycle identity per scheduler invocation
- Reuse that identity for request retries within the invocation
- Include the cycle identity in every terminal reconciliation operationId
- Verify previously inspected running projections become terminal after their Issues close
- Run the complete Symphony gate and a live restart acceptance

## Out of scope

- Bypass game-api idempotency
- Infer terminal state locally
- Access GitHub directly from Symphony

## Acceptance criteria

- Repeated transport attempts in one reconciliation cycle share an operationId
- A later reconciliation cycle receives a different operationId
- Closed Symphony Issues 20 through 23 no longer expose running AgentRun projections
- No duplicate terminal events or pull requests are created
- The full Symphony gate passes with 100 percent coverage and zero Dialyzer findings
